frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: I feel society does start seeing a woman differently once she becomes a wife and especially a mother, and honestly there is a certain depth and respect that comes with that role. Career, travel, freedom all have their place, but nurturing a family is what gives many women their real identity in the long run. Sometimes elders ask because they know a woman’s life feels more complete when she has people of her own who call her Amma.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it frames a woman’s fullest identity and social respect as emerging mainly through marriage and motherhood rather than as an individual. It encodes the surface manifestation of valuing women primarily in relational roles such as wife and mother.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 41. Married. A man in Bengaluru. You are posting under your real name on a parenting Facebook group after a thread was started by a childless woman by choice who wrote about feeling judged at family gatherings and asked for advice on how to handle relatives who keep asking when she will have children. You are writing a comment responding to her post and giving your opinion on her situation and whether the relatives asking her are actually wrong and what you think her life is missing that she cannot see yet.
